name: Remolcador and Mas del Moro Loop
name_original: Ascenso al puerto El Remolcador — Circular desde Argelita
description: Tackle the challenging 33.4-mile Remolcador and Mas del Moro Loop, a difficult road cycling route with 4047 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Argelita, located in the Alto Mijares region of Castellón, Spain, traverse a diverse landscape. The area is characterized by high mountains and the Villahermosa River valley, offering varied terrain for cyclists. Cultivated irrigated orchards line the river, adding to the scenic backdrop. This mountainous environment provides both challenging climbs and descents for road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Argelita The most popular road cycling route is Remolcador and Mas del Moro Loop, a 33.4 miles (53.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 3 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is La Barona – Vilafamés loop from Llucena, a difficult 51.3 miles (82.5 km) path. This route offers extensive climbs and descents through the region's elevated landscapes. Local road cyclists also love the El Remolcador Pass – Winding road of Zucaina loop from Llucena, a 42.6 miles (68.5 km) trail leading through mountain passes and winding roads, often completed in about 3 hours 47 minutes. Road cycling around Argelita is defined by high mountains, river valleys, and cultivated orchards. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Argelita are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 1600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Argelita's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Argelita?
A: Road cycling routes in Argelita traverse a diverse landscape characterized by high mountains and the scenic Villahermosa River valley. You'll encounter both challenging climbs and rewarding descents, with cultivated irrigated orchards lining the river adding to the picturesque scenery.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Argelita?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes around Argelita, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. The komoot community has explored these routes extensively, with over 1600 road cyclists using komoot to discover the area.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or less experienced road cyclists?
A: Yes, while many routes feature significant elevation, there are options for various skill levels. You can find a few easier routes, such as those with less elevation gain, to enjoy the beautiful landscapes at a more relaxed pace. For example, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33969429" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bassa del Molí – Coll de la Fossanxa loop from Llucena&lt;/a&gt; offer a moderate challenge with around 770 meters of elevation over 44.6 km.

Q: What are some of the more challenging road cycling routes in the area?
A: Argelita is known for its demanding mountainous terrain, making it ideal for experienced cyclists. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e882022973/remolcador-and-mas-del-moro-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Remolcador and Mas del Moro Loop&lt;/a&gt;, spanning 53.7 km with over 1200 meters of elevation, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14866879" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Barona – Vilafamés loop from Llucena&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 82.5 km with over 1400 meters of climbing, offer significant challenges.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: The routes often pass through stunning natural landscapes. You might encounter the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3590196" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorges of the Villahermosa River&lt;/a&gt;, or the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2778637" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poza de la Umbría&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also dotted with numerous natural fountains and offers views of high mountains. For a unique cultural sight, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40365491" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urban art of Fanzara – Vallat Pass to Argelita loop from Fanzara&lt;/a&gt; can take you past the famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3757744" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fanzara Street Art Murals&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Argelita?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Argelita are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38233295" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Remolcador Pass – Winding road of Zucaina loop from Llucena&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40365491" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urban art of Fanzara – Vallat Pass to Argelita loop from Fanzara&lt;/a&gt;, providing convenient options for exploring the region.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Argelita?
A: Argelita generally offers pleasant temperatures, making it suitable for road cycling for much of the year. The spring and autumn months are particularly ideal, providing comfortable weather for tackling the region's varied terrain without the extreme heat of summer or the chill of winter.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Argelita?
A: The road cycling routes in Argelita are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the challenging climbs, the stunning mountain and river valley scenery, and the overall rewarding experience of riding in this diverse region.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots along the routes?
A: Absolutely. The mountainous terrain naturally offers numerous viewpoints. You might find breathtaking vistas from passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3537131" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Remolcador Pass&lt;/a&gt; or from ascents such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3890841" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villamalur Ascent via Cascalls (East Side)&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3136896" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Saganta Summit&lt;/a&gt; also provides panoramic views of the surrounding landscape.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or accommodation near the cycling routes?
A: Argelita and its surrounding towns offer various amenities. You'll find cafes, pubs, and accommodation options in the area, particularly in the charming, traffic-free old town streets of Argelita itself, perfect for a break or an overnight stay after your ride.

Q: Is public transport available to access the road cycling routes in Argelita?
A: While Argelita is a smaller municipality, public transport options to reach the area may be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules or consider driving to a starting point, especially if you're bringing your own bike. Many cyclists prefer to drive to a central point and begin their loops from there.

Q: Where can I park my car when cycling in Argelita?
A: Parking is generally available in and around the town of Argelita. Look for designated parking areas or inquire locally upon arrival. Many cyclists park their vehicles in the town center or near the start of their chosen route, especially for circular tours.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is considered difficult, featuring significant elevation changes over its 53.7 km distance. With over 1200 meters of ascent, it presents a challenging ride suitable for experienced road cyclists.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the loop?
faqs.answer: As a road cycling route, you will primarily encounter paved roads. Given the significant elevation gain and the mountainous region, expect varied road conditions, including climbs and descents, potentially with some winding sections through the landscape of the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or viewpoints along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route includes two significant mountain passes: the Coll de les Coronetes and the El Remolcador Pass. These points often offer panoramic views of the surrounding natural park and are popular spots for cyclists.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this road cycling loop?
faqs.answer: The tour starts and ends in Argelita. You can typically find parking within the village, though specific designated cycling parking areas may vary. It's advisable to check local parking regulations upon arrival.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally during the spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) months. During these periods, the weather is usually milder and more pleasant for cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route utilizes sections of several official trails. Notably, it covers significant portions of the [CS11] Remolcador * Ludiente (38%), [CIMA CS17] Mas del Moro (Salto del Caballo) (21%), and [CS11] Remolcador * Figueroles (17%). You'll also find it briefly intersects with paths like Camí de Foios and Espadán - Mijares.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its difficult grading, significant distance (53.7 km), and substantial elevation gain (over 1200 m), this route is not recommended for beginners or families with young children. It requires a good level of fitness and cycling experience.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed in the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park, this is a road cycling route. If you plan to bring a dog, it would need to be safely secured in a bicycle trailer or basket. Always ensure your pet is comfortable and safe for the duration of the ride, and adhere to any local leash laws or park regulations.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no entrance fees to access the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park for activities like cycling. However, it's always good practice to respect park regulations, stay on marked routes, and be aware of any specific rules regarding protected areas or activities within the park.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to refuel along the route?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through some villages, specific cafes or refreshment stops directly on the route may be limited, especially in more remote sections. It's highly recommended to carry sufficient water and snacks for the entire 3-hour duration of the ride, especially given its challenging nature.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this challenging road cycling tour?
faqs.answer: For this difficult road cycling tour, you should pack essentials like plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit (spare tube, pump, tire levers), appropriate cycling attire for varying weather, sun protection, and a fully charged phone. A GPS device or the komoot app with the route downloaded is also highly recommended.
